governance_status: MANUAL_SPLIT_ENFORCEMENT — full orchestration governance not available; inherits parent governance limitations

COMPONENT_A_ONLY.note: Parent threshold 1.0 applied literally to field 1 as standalone level threshold. Degenerate: flags ~0% of rows because no MLB starter averages <= 1.0 IP.

COMPONENT_B_ONLY.note: Parent threshold 1.0 applied literally to field 2 as standalone level threshold. Degenerate: flags ~100% of rows because all MLB starters average >= 1.0 IP.

D01_FULL_FORMULATION.note: Exact parent rule inherited without modification.

structural_ambiguity_note: D01 is a gap rule, not a conjunction of two independent level-threshold flags. The parent threshold (1.0) was designed as a minimum gap between windows, not as a level for either field. Applying it to individual fields produces degenerate component groups. This is the expected structural result, not a test failure.

stability_standard.FORMULATION_REAL: Full formulation at least as stage-stable as both components and meaningfully stronger
stability_standard.COMPONENT_DOMINATED: One component alone explains most of the signal
stability_standard.MIXED_INCONCLUSIVE: No clean dominance pattern or full formulation not clearly more trustworthy
stability_standard.CLOSE: Formulation not structurally trustworthy enough to continue

mechanism_check_result.note: Degenerate components confirm the signal is inherently a gap/trajectory measure, not reducible to either field level. However, this follows from gap-rule structure, not from empirical robustness evidence.

final_verdict_note: Component-dominance decomposition cannot be meaningfully executed for gap rules. Neither component provides a credible alternative. Validation attenuation remains unresolved. D01 is not dismissed but not confirmed as structurally stable.
